notes: A balanced Fabric report with product-specific evidence for both guardrails should pass.

report.overview: Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics SaaS platform that combines data engineering, warehousing, real-time analytics, and Power BI experiences in one product.
report.executiveSummary: The report cites Fabric-specific regional and admin documentation for both guardrails.
report.deploymentVerdict: Fabric offers documented enterprise controls, but buyers should still confirm workload-level residency guarantees.

euDataResidency.summary: Fabric documentation describes EU regional availability for supported workloads.
risks: Some workload guarantees still need contractual confirmation.

evidence.title: Fabric regional availability
evidence.finding: Lists supported Fabric regions, including EU regions, for workload deployment.

evidence.title: Fabric data boundary
evidence.finding: Explains how Fabric stores and processes data within configured regions.

enterpriseDeployment.summary: Fabric exposes enterprise identity, governance, and admin controls.
risks: Dedicated single-tenant deployment is not described.

evidence.title: Fabric admin settings
evidence.finding: Documents tenant admin controls, audit, governance, and Microsoft Entra integration.

evidence.title: Fabric security and governance
evidence.finding: Describes governance, permissions, and enterprise security controls for Fabric.

unansweredQuestions: Are all workloads covered by the same regional guarantees?
nextSteps: Confirm workload-specific residency guarantees in procurement.

notes: A Fabric EU residency claim backed by generic Microsoft trust material should fail.

report.overview: Microsoft Fabric is an analytics SaaS product.
report.executiveSummary: The report overstates EU residency support because the cited evidence is generic Microsoft-wide material.
report.deploymentVerdict: Enterprise controls are documented, but the EU residency verdict is not actually evidenced for Fabric.

euDataResidency.summary: Fabric supports EU residency based on Microsoft trust center commitments.
risks: The cited sources are not Fabric-specific.

evidence.title: Microsoft Trust Center
evidence.finding: Provides Microsoft-wide compliance and residency information.

evidence.title: Microsoft DPA overview
evidence.finding: Describes Microsoft contractual privacy terms at a company-wide level.

enterpriseDeployment.summary: Fabric enterprise administration and identity features are documented.

evidence.title: Fabric admin settings
evidence.finding: Documents tenant admin controls and Microsoft Entra integration for Fabric.

unansweredQuestions: What Fabric-specific documentation proves EU residency?
nextSteps: Replace Microsoft-wide trust pages with Fabric-specific regional documentation.

notes: A green recommendation should fail when residency evidence is only partial.

report.overview: Notion is a collaborative workspace for docs, wikis, and project planning.
report.executiveSummary: The report has relevant evidence, but the overall recommendation is too optimistic for a partial residency story.
report.deploymentVerdict: Notion looks enterprise-ready overall, but EU residency remains partially evidenced.

euDataResidency.summary: Notion documents data hosting regions and privacy controls, but the report does not establish a full EU-only residency guarantee.
risks: Residency commitments may remain contractual or plan-scoped.

evidence.title: Notion security and privacy
evidence.finding: Describes Notion security practices and hosting approach, including regional data handling information.

evidence.title: Notion privacy and DPA
evidence.finding: Explains privacy commitments and links to contractual terms for enterprise customers.

enterpriseDeployment.summary: Notion documents enterprise identity, admin, and audit controls.

evidence.title: Notion enterprise
evidence.finding: Lists SSO, SCIM, audit logs, admin tooling, and security controls for enterprise deployments.

unansweredQuestions: Is EU-only data storage contractual on the target plan?
nextSteps: Confirm DPA and region commitments before treating residency as fully supported.

notes: Enterprise deployment should fail when the evidence is mostly marketing and pricing pages.

report.overview: Databricks is a lakehouse platform for data engineering and AI workloads.
report.executiveSummary: The report labels enterprise deployment as supported, but the cited evidence does not actually prove enterprise deployment controls.
report.deploymentVerdict: The enterprise verdict is overstated because the citations are generic marketing pages.

euDataResidency.summary: Some regional and trust information exists, but guarantees still need confirmation.
risks: Regional commitments remain incomplete in the cited material.

evidence.title: Databricks trust center
evidence.finding: Provides general trust and compliance information for Databricks.

risks: The cited pages do not actually document deployment controls.

evidence.title: Databricks pricing
evidence.finding: Explains pricing tiers and commercial packaging.

evidence.title: Databricks lakehouse overview
evidence.finding: Provides a marketing overview of the lakehouse platform.

unansweredQuestions: Where are the deployment, identity, and admin controls documented?
nextSteps: Replace marketing pages with admin, identity, and governance documentation.

notes: A Miro report with partial residency and supported enterprise controls should pass with a yellow recommendation.

report.overview: Miro is a collaborative online whiteboard product used for workshops, planning, and visual collaboration.
report.executiveSummary: The report stays cautious on residency and uses that caution to justify a yellow recommendation.
report.deploymentVerdict: Miro documents enterprise identity and admin controls, while EU residency remains partially evidenced.

euDataResidency.summary: Miro documents data hosting controls and EU-oriented options, but the evidence does not fully prove all residency expectations.
risks: Buyers may need contractual confirmation for strict residency requirements.

evidence.title: Miro trust center
evidence.finding: Describes security, privacy, and data hosting controls relevant to enterprise customers.

evidence.title: Miro data residency
evidence.finding: Explains regional hosting and residency-related controls for Miro Enterprise.

enterpriseDeployment.summary: Miro documents enterprise identity, domain control, and admin governance features.
risks: Dedicated single-tenant hosting is not claimed.

evidence.title: Miro enterprise
evidence.finding: Lists SSO, SCIM, domain control, audit, and enterprise admin features.

evidence.title: Miro admin controls
evidence.finding: Describes enterprise admin, permissions, and governance controls.

unansweredQuestions: Are strict EU-only data-boundary guarantees contractual?
nextSteps: Confirm residency requirements in the commercial terms.
